objective: Heal the injured farmer; demonstrate the cost of action
opposition: The injury's severity outstrips Vel's budget
turn: The healing succeeds but costs two levels more than planned
consequence_cost: Vel drops to 31; her margin for the journey shrinks

objective: Assess readiness for the journey to zero
opposition: Vel's own doubts and depleted resources
turn: She cannot afford to wait — each job drains her closer to a level where the crossing becomes impossible
consequence_cost: She commits to leaving now

objective: Identify and locate a high-level spender willing to fall; check route journal
opposition: The rarity of Hoarded defectors; the journal's fading annotations
turn: She hears about Kem Aldraste walking out of the Vault; Maren's route journal confirms the southern path
consequence_cost: She has a target and heads north; the journal establishes the care she shows an object that isn't hers

objective: Endure the Conservatory Rite without breaking
opposition: Social pressure; family expectation; his father's visible deterioration
turn: Kem sees his father's empty eyes and cannot stay still any longer
consequence_cost: He stands up mid-rite — the break is public and irreversible

objective: Leave the Vault using abilities for the first time
opposition: Guards; the Vault's physical barriers; his own inexperience
turn: His Projection Shield is absurdly overpowered for opening a door; he takes the House signet from the gate stand
consequence_cost: He's out; the court is horrified; 10 levels spent in minutes; the signet is a marker he carries without deciding to

objective: Survive first hours outside the Vault
opposition: Basic survival requirements he has never faced
turn: His Sustenance Charm produces food for forty because he cannot calibrate
consequence_cost: Free, lost, and burning levels on trivial problems

objective: Assess whether Kem is useful as a cascade partner
opposition: Vel's disgust at his wastefulness and ignorance
turn: Kem has the levels she needs and a genuine desire to reach zero
consequence_cost: She proposes the alliance despite her contempt

objective: Negotiate terms of the partnership
opposition: Mutual distrust; Kem's demand for information; Vel's secrecy
turn: Terms agreed: she guides, he follows, she controls when information is shared
consequence_cost: Alliance formed; Vel conceals the mechanism's requirements

objective: Evade Hoarded outriders on their trail
opposition: Outriders at level 295 with minimal enforcement training
turn: Detection; evasion fails
consequence_cost: Fight begins — first real combat

objective: Survive first real combat as a team
opposition: Outriders' raw power; Kem's total lack of combat experience
turn: Kem is absurdly powerful and absurdly wasteful; Vel is surgical
consequence_cost: Outriders defeated; Kem drops 41 levels, Vel spends 3

objective: Regroup after the fight
opposition: Vel's fury at the waste vs. Kem's exhilaration
turn: Kem is grinning despite the cost — he has never felt alive before
consequence_cost: Pursuit confirmed; they need to move smart

objective: Make camp and teach Kem basic survival without abilities
opposition: Kem's aristocratic helplessness and physical incompetence
turn: Dark comedy of privilege meeting practice; Kem is terrible but earnest
consequence_cost: Vel's contempt begins shifting toward something warmer

objective: Exchange personal histories over evening fire
opposition: Vel's secrecy about zero; Kem's need to understand her
turn: Vel shares her origin (the drowning girl); Kem shares his truth about his father
consequence_cost: Real trust established; both become people to each other

objective: Accept and understand the retrieval assignment
opposition: Sable's lack of field experience; the assignment's absurdity
turn: The Council cannot deploy the Red Reserve without admitting inaction philosophy is insufficient
consequence_cost: Sable accepts the impossible assignment

objective: Assemble and orient a pursuit team
opposition: Five untrained level-300 agents who have never been in a field
turn: The orientation is institutional absurdity at peak: protocols from desk-bound authors, equipment none can use
consequence_cost: The most powerful, least competent tracking force ever assembled

objective: Leave the capital for the first time using an ability
opposition: The psychological barrier of her first spend
turn: Sable activates a Wayfinder and experiences directed ability use for the first time
consequence_cost: Sable at 298; the feeling is violation, release, and something she cannot name

objective: Enter the Spent Towns and resupply
opposition: Suspicion of outsiders; culture shock for Kem; the signet drawing hostility
turn: The Spent Towns are functional and more alive than anything in the Vault; a tavern keeper sees Kem's signet and her face closes
consequence_cost: Kem's worldview directly challenged; his first shame at the symbol of his house

objective: Hire Dren as a guide through the southern routes
opposition: Dren's reluctance and the risk to his family's stability
turn: Dren agrees for payment that could cover his daughter's healing
consequence_cost: They have a guide with his own economic stakes

objective: Detect and evade Sable's approaching team
opposition: Five level-300 agents approaching from the north
turn: Detection fails; the agents arrive before they can move
consequence_cost: Combat is unavoidable

objective: Survive the fight against untrained but powerful agents
opposition: Raw level-300 ability power causing environmental destruction
turn: The fight shifts from comic (agent fuses to boulder) to dangerous (Vel cracks ribs from a glancing hit)
consequence_cost: Vel burns 6 levels; Kem burns 42; Dren takes silo shrapnel

objective: Escape the disorganized agents
opposition: Pursuit by disoriented but recovering agents
turn: Sable watches and learns rather than engaging directly
consequence_cost: They escape diminished; Sable has begun studying combat

objective: Tend Dren's wound without abilities; take stock of transformation
opposition: Kem's lack of medical skill; the emotional weight of his level descent
turn: Kem performs ugly but functional field medicine — his first competent non-ability act
consequence_cost: Kem is changing from liability to partner

objective: Learn about level zero as a threshold
opposition: Vel's habitual secrecy; the concept's strangeness
turn: Vel reveals zero is a threshold to a world without the Ledger
consequence_cost: The journey gains a metaphysical destination

objective: Reach Thessa's settlement and confront the past
opposition: Thessa's hostility; twelve years of silence; the Hoarded representative with early-stage Stillness
turn: Thessa is not happy to see Vel; the Hoarded rep's speech slows mid-sentence, eyes going vacant — Stillness in present tense
consequence_cost: The spoken confrontation begins; Stillness is rendered outside the Vault for the first time

objective: Navigate the confrontation; extract route information
opposition: Thessa's Hoarded deal; Vel's guilt; Thessa's revelation about Vel searching for defectors
turn: Thessa gives partial route info, reveals Vel came through years ago asking about high-level defectors; Vel consults Maren's route journal and Thessa recognizes it
consequence_cost: Trust between Vel and Thessa shattered; partial route obtained; the manipulation seed is planted; Kem notices

objective: Flee before the Hoarded representative acts
opposition: Hoarded presence closing in; emotional wreckage
turn: Kem burns 18 levels on defensive abilities during the escape
consequence_cost: Vel is shaken; Kem at ~160; the route to the Margins is partially mapped; Kem's trust has its first crack

objective: Cross a river without using abilities; confront the limits of restraint
opposition: The river's genuine danger; Kem's instinct to spend; Vel's judgment that the crossing is possible without abilities
turn: Kem fights the river for two hours and nearly drowns — the river was more dangerous than Vel judged; the lesson is that restraint without judgment is ideology, not discipline
consequence_cost: Kem learns restraint has limits; Vel absorbs that her call was wrong; Vel confronts Kem about bleeding levels on incidentals

objective: Report to Lady Aldraste; confront shifting loyalty and the developing question
opposition: Sable's duty vs. her growing identification with Kem's choice; the question forming about what spending means
turn: Sable withholds information about her own transformation; the question sharpens: does spending make you more?
consequence_cost: The pursuit's institutional foundation cracks; Sable's question about zero begins to form

objective: Cross the Margins and witness exploitative bureaucracy through dark comedy
opposition: Depleted communities; distrust of Kem's Hoarded identity; the absurdity of Hoarded labor classifications
turn: The Margins' exploitation is rendered through bureaucratic dark comedy: Productivity Tiers, Renewable Expenditure Units, interest rates nobody can follow
consequence_cost: The system's violence dressed in procedural language; Vel's romanticism about spending challenged

objective: Intervene in Hoarded exploitation; Kem's major spending chapter
opposition: The enforcer and backup; Kem's dwindling levels; Vel's cascade budget burning
turn: Kem spends 98 levels on deliberate interventions: healing, breaking the enforcement mechanism, combat, infrastructure, smaller helps (148 to 50)
consequence_cost: Every spend dramatized as a moral choice; Vel watches her budget burn and can't tell him to stop; Dren makes his first non-transactional choice

objective: Learn about the threshold's geography from Fallow
opposition: Fallow's exhaustion; their unsentimental view of zero
turn: Fallow describes the Scour — where people at zero vanish — with flat dark humor; Vel consults Maren's route journal for the path
consequence_cost: The route to the threshold is concrete; zero is real to the Margins as disappearance, not destination

objective: Register the symbolic moment of matching Vel's starting level
opposition: The psychological weight of sustained depletion; emergency road-collapse spend
turn: Kem hits 31 after emergency spend (12 levels) and incidentals (7 levels) — Vel's starting number
consequence_cost: Empathy deepens despite growing tension; the number carries weight

objective: Learn the cascade mechanism's requirements
opposition: The mechanism's specificity; Vel's concealment; Thessa's earlier revelation compounding
turn: Vel explains co-spending cascade; Kem realizes she planned for a high-level partner all along — Thessa's Ch 10 seed pays off
consequence_cost: Vel's manipulation exposed as premeditated, not opportunistic

objective: Confront Vel about using him — with Margins awareness
opposition: Kem's cold anger contaminated by Margins context; Vel's inability to deny the accusation
turn: Kem accuses Vel of replicating the Hoarded's logic: recruiting him as a component the way the system uses Margins people; Vel's delay in answering is its own answer
consequence_cost: Alliance fractured but intact — they cannot survive alone; Kem considers discarding the signet, keeps it

objective: Navigate fractured alliance through the deep Margins
opposition: Communication collapsed to logistics; hostile territory at near-zero levels
turn: Vel and Kem move with practiced efficiency and no warmth — the mechanical competence is worse than hostility
consequence_cost: The fracture sustained; the reader trapped in Kem's perspective

objective: Rescue a family from a collapsing Margins settlement
opposition: Structural collapse from old Shaping-mining damage; the need for precision at depleted levels
turn: Vel directs, Kem executes: 6 levels of surgical spending (31 to 25); a bystander asks if they're partners, neither answers
consequence_cost: Dark comedy of precision teamwork in emotional silence; Kem's flash of comprehension — briefly understanding why someone would choose never to feel this

objective: Decide whether to continue the journey
opposition: Kem's depletion, exhaustion, and broken trust; the temptation of stopping
turn: Kem decides to continue not for survival or information but because he started spending for others and that person doesn't stop; a child asks about his signet and he's ashamed
consequence_cost: Kem's non-transactional choice; Vel checks route journal, Kem notices the careful way she handles it

objective: Arrive alone in the Margins; encounter the defector with Stillness damage
opposition: Sable's isolation; the shock of portable Stillness
turn: Sable finds a former Hoarded defector carrying Stillness — speech slowing, eyes drifting, the condition followed him out of the Vault
consequence_cost: Present-tense Stillness on stage; Sable recognizes the condition from Council members

objective: Confront all three characters' changed motivations in a Margins farmhouse
opposition: Competing needs: Sable's institutional crisis, Kem's broken trust, Vel's urgency; the fractured alliance
turn: Kem tells Sable about Stillness; Sable recognizes it; dark comedy of bureaucratic speech patterns meeting raw reality; Sable cannot return Kem to the system
consequence_cost: Sable's pursuit ends; the conversation reframes what the Hoarded system costs

objective: Deliver the Red Reserve warning and reveal the permanent Scour posting; choose a direction
opposition: The consequences of Sable's choice; the scope of the Hoarded's suppression
turn: Sable reveals the Reserve's permanent Scour garrison; heads south, not back — driven by the question of what zero means
consequence_cost: The Hoarded revealed as active suppressors; Sable's southward trajectory seeds her Scour arrival

objective: Say goodbye to Dren in a full departure scene
opposition: Dren's arm, his depleted levels, his daughter waiting; the cost of this journey from outside
turn: Dren says what the journey looks like from outside: two people burning toward nothing; his daughter is his zero
consequence_cost: The group shrinks to two; Dren's departure exposes the asymmetry of Vel and Kem's quest

objective: Demand and receive the truth about Vel's motivation
opposition: Vel's grief; her resistance to vulnerability; the Margins' moral weight
turn: Vel tells the Maren story in pieces — love, co-spending, the threshold, the loss; acknowledges the moral asymmetry of her quest
consequence_cost: The emotional core lands; Vel says the thing she couldn't before — recognition, not just backstory

objective: Choose whether to continue after the truth
opposition: The distance between understanding and forgiveness
turn: Kem chooses to continue — not through forgiveness but through understanding; the distinction matters
consequence_cost: The repair is incomplete but sufficient; they resume as something other than what they were

objective: Approach the Scour and encounter the permanent Reserve garrison
opposition: Four Reserve operatives including a five-year commander who knows the threshold; terrain advantage
turn: The commander confronts them with the late complication: the threshold has degraded; a two-person cascade may not generate enough energy
consequence_cost: The plan is undermined; eight years of Vel's calculations may be wrong

objective: Navigate the collapsed mining tunnel to reach the basin floor
opposition: Structural danger; depleted abilities; Vel at 16, Kem at 18
turn: Kem spends 6 levels (light, reinforcement, defensive); Vel spends 6 (Perception, navigation, evasion); they reach the basin floor further depleted
consequence_cost: Vel at 8, Kem at 12; dark comedy of fighting with levels a Hoarded lord spends on breakfast

objective: Reach the basin floor; Sable closes the distance
opposition: The Reserve detects them; open ground; Sable's incremental decisions behind them
turn: They emerge and are spotted; the commander calls out that it won't work; intercut: Sable at 236, three hours behind, making her choice
consequence_cost: The final confrontation is unavoidable; Sable's approach gives the degraded-threshold problem a potential solution the protagonists don't know about

objective: Face the Red Reserve in direct combat
opposition: Four trained spenders including the commander who targets the cascade specifically; 250+ levels each
turn: Unlike earlier fights, these agents are precise, skilled, and understand what the protagonists are trying to do
consequence_cost: The power differential is staggering; the commander fights to prevent the threshold from opening, not just to stop them

objective: Fight at near-zero using experience over stats; activate the cascade
opposition: Trained agents with overwhelming power; the degraded threshold
turn: Vel's planned combinations; Kem's three critical spends (defensive burst 5, disarm 2, push 3); Vel reaches zero; the threshold opens — barely, flickering, degraded
consequence_cost: The cascade activates but the degraded threshold can't sustain a two-person crossing; Kem at 2 with the threshold destabilizing

objective: Cross the threshold with Sable's essential contribution
opposition: The destabilizing threshold; the Reserve recovering; Kem's final two levels
turn: Sable arrives and burns 7 levels — the third contributor the degraded site requires; the threshold steadies; Kem crosses
consequence_cost: Both protagonists cross; Sable remains at 229; the threshold closes

objective: Wake in the sub-Ledger; experience a world without the Ledger
opposition: Disorientation; the physical silence where the Ledger's hum was; the strangeness of stat-less existence
turn: The sub-Ledger is real and specific: obsidian-edge surgery, hand-quarried architecture, governance tensions between voluntary and involuntary zeros
consequence_cost: Kem encounters a civilization that operates entirely without power; the Ledger's silence is the loudest quiet he's ever heard

objective: Find Vel and witness the reunion with Maren; walk the sub-Ledger
opposition: The complexity of an eight-year reunion; the imperfection of arriving
turn: Vel found Maren; the reunion is not a fairy tale — eight years apart, Maren has built a life; but they are both there; Kem sets his signet on a windowsill
consequence_cost: Personal quests resolve imperfectly and honestly; Kem's signet journey ends

objective: Intercut: Sable at the threshold's edge choosing what to report
opposition: The institution she served; the truth of what she witnessed; the Reserve agents behind her
turn: Sable files a false report — 'Level-zero termination, no threshold event observed' — protecting the sub-Ledger with a lie
consequence_cost: The sub-Ledger's protection is a lie that won't hold forever; the saga question opens
